payload.title: Add database-aware readiness check with minimal targeted code and test edits
payload.approach: Patch only `HealthService.readiness()` and extend its unit tests, then validate via the existing npm build and test commands without touching deployment behavior.
payload.rationale: This confines change impact to routing-relevant readiness logic, preserving all public contracts while preventing traffic from reaching DB-unhealthy pods.

acceptanceCoverage.strategy: Change readiness to require both `this.processState.running` and `await this.db.isAvailable()` and leave the method signature unchanged.
Catch rejections from `isAvailable()` in readiness and return `false` deterministically on any error.
Do not modify `liveness` or `startup` method signatures and only alter behavior if strictly necessary to satisfy DB-gated readiness.
Add focused tests in `test/health-service.test.ts` covering readiness false when DB is unavailable and true when DB is available while process state is running.
Validate `npm run build` and `npm test` pass after the change to confirm strict TypeScript compile and test expectations still hold.

invariantProtection.strategy: Keep `HealthService` and `DatabaseHealth` public types and method contracts intact so existing callers compile without source-level compatibility changes.
Do not add any production dependencies and keep changes compatible with strict TypeScript settings in `tsconfig.json`.
Restrict behavior change to readiness readiness semantics only, leaving startup/liveness contracts and non-health-routing behavior untouched.
Avoid editing `deployment.json`; if probe configuration is considered for later, do so only with explicit approval.

files.purpose: Implement minimal readiness gating using database availability while preserving the existing API.
Add deterministic tests for DB-available and DB-unavailable readiness behavior.
Use existing `build` and `test` scripts as-is for validation entry points.
Read for verification only to confirm readiness is the routing-relevant probe.

steps.description: Review current health service readiness, startup, and liveness implementations and confirm where database availability is currently unused.
Update `HealthService.readiness()` to await `database.isAvailable()` only for readiness and return false on errors while preserving method signature.
Extend readiness tests with explicit DB-unavailable and DB-error cases and keep existing liveness/startup tests intact.
Run non-interactive build and test verification commands and report pass/fail with any adjustment needed to keep strict mode satisfied.

verificationCommands.name: npm run build
verificationCommands.purpose: Validate strict TypeScript compilation after readiness logic changes.

verificationCommands.name: npm test
verificationCommands.purpose: Execute full repository test suite including health-service unit coverage.

risks: Readiness may flap under slow or intermittently failing DB health checks unless probe timing and retry behavior are already appropriate for the environment.
Adding DB dependency to readiness may reduce observed availability during short transient DB failures if readiness thresholds are too strict.
assumptions: Readiness probe outcomes in `deployment.json` are treated as the primary traffic-gating signal for routing.
`DatabaseHealth.isAvailable()` is safe and inexpensive enough for readiness invocations and can fail fast when DB is unavailable.

unknowns.description: Whether upstream infrastructure exclusively relies on Kubernetes readiness and ignores other routing paths remains unverified in-repo.
unknowns.resolution: Keep the change limited to readiness and confirm with platform ownership after implementation.

unknowns.description: Any service-mesh or gateway-level health logic outside this repository could override readiness behavior for traffic decisions.
unknowns.resolution: Coordinate with deployment/infrastructure stakeholders if routing still occurs during DB downtime.

recovery: If tests fail, revert readiness changes to process-only logic and reintroduce DB gating with stricter, deterministic mocking or defensive error handling.
If build fails, revert or isolate strictness violations in `src/health-service.ts` only, then re-run `npm run build` and `npm test`.
